The first product from Virgil Abloh's archive just hit shelves and it is PERFECT.

The Virgil Abloh Archive x Air Jordan 1 High OG Alaska released globally yesterday at $230 and sold out at every major retailer within minutes. The shoe is built on the foundation of the original Air Jordan 1 Off White Euro colourway that Virgil created as part of his landmark 2017 Nike collaboration known as The Ten. All white deconstructed leather with semi detached panels and a Nike Swoosh attached with exposed blue stitching. The iconic Nike Air text appears in orange on the elongated foam tongue. The word AIR runs across the midsole in block lettering. This marks the first global product from the Virgil Abloh Archive which was established to continue his creative vision after his passing in November 2021. Resale prices are already hitting $500 plus on StockX.

Virgil changed sneaker culture forever and this shoe is proof that his ideas were so ahead they still feel like the future four years later.

caption_en:
­ЪЋи№ИЈ Jordan 1 Retro High OG РђюNext ChapterРђЮ ­ЪЋИ№ИЈ

The Jordan 1 РђюNext ChapterРђЮ is not a reinterpreted Chicago. It is a multiverse built in the form of J1.

Unlike Origin Story, which was a cleaner homage to the classic, this version takes much more risks. Here each panel seems to come from a different dimension. It does not seek uniformity, it seeks concept.

In terms of materials, it is one of the most varied Jordan 1s in recent years. It combines bright red patent leather with smooth white leather, matte red panels and areas with a glossy finish. The collar introduces a textile with a comic-type pattern, while the black swoosh has a shiny effect that, in the light, reveals a slight iridescent effect. The OG-style cream nylon tongue finishes tying everything up.

An interesting detail is on the tongue. The РђюNike AirРђЮ appears only at the front, without extending towards the rear like on other OGs. It's subtle, but it changes the classic reading of the model.

The glow in the dark sole adds a lot. When charged with light, the bottom emits that characteristic green glow in the dark, fitting perfectly with the energy of the pair.

The box maintains the OG red and black format. After such a well-worked concept, one could imagine something different... but perhaps in another universe that version does exist.

The Jordan 1 РђюNext ChapterРђЮ is a conceptual, bold and narrative pair. A J1 that makes it clear that there is no single version of the hero.

Jordan 1 РђюBaroque BrownРђЮ 

Jordan Brand continues to affirm its position with the enchanting "Baroque Brown" color scheme on the Air Jordan 1 line. The warm brown color is harmoniously combined with neutral colors, creating an appearance that is both classic and liberal. This is definitely an indispensable item in the shoe wardrobe of sneakerheads who love vintage style mixed with a bit of modernity.

3 ЯИбЯИ▒ЯИЂЯИЕЯ╣їЯ╣ЃЯИФЯИЇЯ╣ѕЯ╣ЂЯИФЯ╣ѕЯИЄЯИДЯИЄЯИЂЯИ▓ЯИБЯИфЯИЋЯИБЯИхЯИЌЯ╣ЂЯИДЯИБЯ╣ї (Fragment Design x Union LA x Jordan) ЯИЌЯИхЯ╣ѕЯИфЯИБЯ╣ЅЯИ▓ЯИЄЯИёЯИДЯИ▓ЯИАЯИ«ЯИиЯИГЯИ«ЯИ▓ЯИЌЯИхЯ╣ѕЯИфЯИИЯИћЯ╣ЃЯИЎЯИЏЯИх 2026 ЯИёЯИБЯИ▒ЯИџ Я╣ѓЯИћЯИбЯИАЯИ▓Я╣ЃЯИЎЯИБЯИФЯИ▒ЯИфЯИфЯИ┤ЯИЎЯИёЯ╣ЅЯИ▓ IO7847-002 ЯИЂЯИ▒ЯИџЯИёЯИ╣Я╣ѕЯИфЯИхЯИБЯИ░ЯИћЯИ▒ЯИџЯИЋЯИ│ЯИЎЯИ▓ЯИЎЯИГЯИбЯ╣ѕЯИ▓ЯИЄ 'Varsity Red'

I had everyone look at it at a gathering of experts, and the quality of the fakes has improved so much that it's hard to judge unless you have the original to compare it to.
There are three major differences between the fakes and the originals that I've seen so far. I think there are individual differences and differences between the factories, but the ankle is constricted when viewed from the back.
The fake one has a short body, while the original one has a curvature like a Coca-Cola bottle.
It's possible that some fake companies have improved their skills and are able to pass this point.
Fake has shallow wing mark embossment.
The fake is just one piece of leather pressed against the leather, whereas the original has a bulge that looks like there's a sponge inside? It has a embossed feel.
Finally, the smell of Bond.
Definitely different.
